title: micromeria fruticosa var. italica
intro: Micromeria fruticosa var. italica, commonly known as the Corsican mint, is a perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region, belonging to the Lamiaceae family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues, respiratory problems, and as an antiseptic. The plant is rich in essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in alleviating symptoms of colds, fever, and digestive discomfort. Herbal preparations of Micromeria fruticosa var. italica can be made into teas, infusions, or used in combination with other herbs in traditional medicine, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for therapeutic purposes.
